behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is primarily rule logic (threshold comparison + category-specific config lookup), which can fail inside one model boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No AI output dependency and no agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: Integration/System/Agentic were considered and rejected because behavior text is strictly deterministic rule scoring ('configured amount threshold for its merchant category').
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function/model with an in-memory config containing per-category thresholds and a transaction above its category threshold; assert exact numeric score change and threshold-rule attribution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test is fully executable as a local function/object test; higher levels are unnecessary for this behavior.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: Correctness depends on model judgment over contextual pattern quality, not deterministic wrapper logic. A stubbed model would always hide true misclassification risk. REAL-INFRA may exist, but the higher failure boundary is model-output validity. MULTI-STEP: Single model decision, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a labeled scenario set containing cross-geo rapid small-purchase fraud patterns where rule thresholds stay below limits; assert fraud probability/ranking exceeds decision threshold with acceptable recall on this slice.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The assertion is about model decision quality; only a real model call can catch the failure.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Typical failure appears at module handoff (service -> transaction store/query/time-window boundary semantics), not just arithmetic. REAL-INFRA: A local in-process DB/repo is sufficient; no need for real network/OS primitives. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not AI-driven, no agent chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text only requires 'counts ... within configured time window per user' and does not require real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring service to a real local repository (or embedded DB), seed transactions across boundary timestamps for multiple users, run velocity check, and assert per-user counts respect inclusive/exclusive window config exactly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Catching boundary/query integration bugs requires module wiring; unit-only fixtures may miss repository/window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is in orchestration/state handoff between model outputs and review-hold decision path. Unit could test boolean logic, but real regressions often occur in wiring between model result objects and decision service. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) correctness here does not depend on real model quality because inputs are explicit flag states, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run decision/orchestration modules together with deterministic model-result fixtures (risk=true, score=false) and assert transaction state transitions to 'held_for_review' with correct reason metadata.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is needed for cross-module output mapping; higher levels are not required by this behavior text.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is contract/aggregation behavior across scoring output, risk output, and response assembler; failures often occur in field mapping/serialization between modules. REAL-INFRA: No real external OS/network requirement. LLM-DEP: Does not test model quality, only presence/correct propagation of fields. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the aggregation path with both model modules wired (or realistic adapters), produce a combined decision payload, and assert schema includes score, fraud_probability, per-model flag indicators, and explanation provenance fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A pure unit formatter test may miss module contract mismatches; integration catches real field handoff failures.
